name: Control Weather

effects.description: You take control of the weather within 5 miles of you for the duration. You must be outdoors to cast this spell, and it ends early if you go indoors. When you cast the spell, you change the current weather conditions, which are determined by the DM. You can change precipitation, temperature, and wind. It takes 1d4 x 10 minutes for the new conditions to take effect. Once they do so, you can change the conditions again. When the spell ends, the weather gradually returns to normal. When you change the weather conditions, find a current condition on the following tables and change its stage by one, up or down. When changing the wind, you can change its direction.

controlOptions.name: Precipitation
controlOptions.effect: The caster can change precipitation by one stage up or down from the current DM-determined condition.

controlOptions.name: Temperature
controlOptions.effect: The caster can change temperature by one stage up or down from the current DM-determined condition.

controlOptions.name: Wind
controlOptions.effect: The caster can change wind by one stage up or down and can change wind direction.

controlOptions.name: Delayed weather change
controlOptions.effect: New conditions take effect after 1d4 x 10 minutes.

controlOptions.name: Stage shift
controlOptions.effect: Weather changes move one stage up or down on the relevant current-condition table.

controlOptions.name: Indoors ending
controlOptions.effect: The spell must be cast outdoors and ends early if the caster goes indoors.
controlOptions.details: When the spell ends, weather gradually returns to normal.

aiContext.prompt: Ask the DM for current precipitation, temperature, and wind stages. Let the caster move one stage up or down, including wind direction, with changes taking effect after 1d4 x 10 minutes.
